title: Structured Techniques Toolkit — authoring and refinement workspace for disciplined analytic instruments

fields.vision: A studio for converting structured analytic techniques into usable instruments.
fields.mechanism: Each technique needs purpose, inputs, steps, failure modes, interpretation rules, reviewer actions, and example outputs, including adaptations for human analysts, AI systems, and human-AI collaboration. The studio supports authoring brand-new SATs, refining and tweaking existing SATs, binding SATs to a corpus, cross-referencing the Analyst Workstation SAT surfaces, and reconciling Beebe/Pherson-style handbooks and tradecraft primers into usable method records.
fields.built_components: The construction workspace renders on the public site with the three workflow lanes (authoring, refinement, binding) and the cross-reference to the live SAT page. The canonical SAT corpus is present as the construction workspace. Open work: the binding from individual SATs to their specific Heuer / Beebe-Pherson passages has not yet been wired through the source-register pipeline.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Wire the SAT-corpus binding through the source-register pipeline so every SAT in the construction workspace traces to a specific admitted passage. Once the binding lands, the construction workspace becomes the place new SATs are authored against checkable source rather than against the canon-as-name. Step 01 Source-register binding Each SAT in the construction workspace threaded through the source-register pipeline. Step 02 SAT-to-passage trace Every SAT traceable to a specific admitted passage in the canonical primer. Step 03 Author-against-source page New SATs authored against checkable source rather than against the canon-as-name.
fields.status: Status: The construction workspace renders on the public site with the three workflow lanes (authoring, refinement, binding) and the cross-reference to the live SAT page. The canonical SAT corpus is present as the construction workspace. Open work: the binding from individual SATs to their specific Heuer / Beebe-Pherson passages has not yet been wired through the source-register pipeline.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: wire the SAT-corpus binding through the source-register pipeline so every SAT in the construction workspace traces to a specific admitted passage. Once the binding lands, the construction workspace becomes the place new SATs are authored against checkable source rather than against the canon-as-name.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Where new structured-analytic techniques are authored from the canonical literature. The page's premise: a SAT is not a label, it is a constructed instrument that can be checked against its source authorities. This is the instrument-building page: every newly authored SAT is bound to its source by construction rather than by claim. It is distinct from the Structured Methods Library, which catalogs method families; this toolkit is where methods become usable analytic instruments.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: Three workflow lanes coexist in the construction workspace: authoring brand-new SATs, refining the existing canon, and binding either back to the source corpus. Authoring New SAT specification page Where a new structured-analytic technique is authored — the question it answers, the analytical move it makes, the failure modes it guards against, the canonical authority it builds on.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A studio for converting structured analytic techniques into usable instruments.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: Each technique needs purpose, inputs, steps, failure modes, interpretation rules, reviewer actions, and example outputs, including adaptations for human analysts, AI systems, and human-AI collaboration. The studio supports authoring brand-new SATs, refining and tweaking existing SATs, binding SATs to a corpus, cross-referencing the Analyst Workstation SAT surfaces, and reconciling Beebe/Pherson-style handbooks and tradecraft primers into usable method records.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It shows method-building skill: not just applying ACH or red-team ideas, but turning every SAT into a reusable, testable research and review tool with explicit inputs, operating rules, and failure modes.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It shows method-building skill: not just applying ACH or red-team ideas, but turning every SAT into a reusable, testable research and review tool with explicit inputs, operating rules, and failure modes.

instruments.input: intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.operation: selects the structured analytic technique or technique sequence that fits the task rather than applying SAT labels ornamentally
instruments.output: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: The construction workspace renders on the public site with the three workflow lanes (authoring, refinement, binding) and the cross-reference to the.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role and produce method assignment and technique rationale as a.
instruments.verification_check: Run one analytic question through Task And Method Assignment; confirm evidence, assumptions, method decision, confidence limit, and human/AI delta.
